title: Critonia sexangularis
intro: Critonia sexangularis, also known as Yerba de la Muerta, is a plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Critonia sexangularis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a decoction, tea, or infusion, and is also used in herbal remedies such as tinctures, salves, and ointments.
taxonomy: Critonia sexangularis is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It is native to the Americas and is a member of the tribe Heliantheae, which includes many other plants with similar characteristics. Critonia sexangularis is closely related to other species within the same tribe, and its classification is based on its morphological and molecular characteristics. The plant's classification is further supported by its unique features, such as its sexangular leaves and small yellow flowers. Critonia sexangularis is a relatively understudied species, and further research is needed to fully understand its classification and relationships within the plant kingdom.
